sections.paragraph:
Original: שׂבמה שׂבם

Transliteration: śebâm śibmâh

Phonetic: seb-awm'

BDB Definition:

Shebam or Shibmah or Sibmah = "fragrance"
  1. one of the towns in the pastoral district on the east of the Jordan in Moab; allotted to the tribes of Reuben and Gad

Origin: probably from H1313

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
